title: New parent in the metro
description: You just had a kid in a busy metro city and need a five-seater that ferries the family without making weekend parking a nightmare. Top safety and an automatic gearbox for stop-go traffic are non-negotiable.
highlights: Five seats with ISOFIX child-seat mounts
Under 4m length so metro parking stays easy

title: First-time buyer in Tier 2/3
tagline: Cheap to run, easy to fix anywhere
description: You're upgrading from a two-wheeler in a smaller town. The car needs to be cheap, sip fuel, and have a service centre on every other street so a roadside breakdown isn't a disaster.
highlights: Service network in every district town
Light clutch and easy controls for new drivers

title: Weekend warrior
description: Weekdays you potter through traffic, but on Friday night you hit broken roads and ghats with mates. You want SUV stance, decent ground clearance and an automatic so you can sip coffee while crawling out of the city.

title: Highway commuter
tagline: Cruise home in comfort, every single day
description: You rack up 60-80km on highway every day. You want a refined diesel or hybrid, an automatic for the daily slog, and the kind of seats that don't leave your back screaming at 9pm.
highlights: Long-haul comfort seats and big boot

title: Young pro, flying solo
tagline: Looks-first daily — sharp, slick, low maintenance
description: Single, mid-twenties, working in a Tier-1 city. You don't need seven seats — you need a sharp-looking petrol automatic that screams 'I made it' in the office basement.
highlights: Compact enough for valet parking anywhere
Designs that turn heads at the office

title: Premium upgrader
tagline: Stepping up — the next car is the statement
description: You've owned a hatchback for six years. The next car must feel like a clear upgrade — German build, 5-star safety, and an SUV or sedan that looks at home in a five-star valet line.
highlights: Powerful turbo-petrol or refined diesel automatic

title: EV-curious metro buyer
tagline: Plug in, glide silently through gridlock
description: You live in a metro with home charging available and you're ready to try going electric — or at least a strong hybrid. You'll stretch the budget for the right battery, but tight parking and an automatic-only commute are the deal-makers.
highlights: Lower running costs vs. petrol equivalent

title: Seven-seater, multi-generational family
tagline: Room for kids, in-laws, and the weekend trip
description: Three generations under one roof — kids in school, parents at home, in-laws over for the holidays. You need genuine seven-seat space, ground clearance for the village run, and an automatic for the daily school commute.
highlights: Genuine 7-seat layout with usable third row
200mm+ ground clearance for unbroken village roads
4-star+ safety to protect the whole family

title: Fleet cabbie / commercial driver
tagline: 200km a day — make every paisa count
description: You drive 200km+ every day for a living, ferrying passengers between airport, office and home. Running cost is everything — diesel or hybrid, manual is fine, and you'd rather pocket the savings on a 3-star car than splurge on safety extras you'll never feel.
highlights: Diesel or strong-hybrid for the lowest per-km cost
Spacious sedan/MUV body for paying passengers
Proven service network and cheap spares

title: Compact second car
tagline: Tiny budget, just for the school run
description: The household already has a main car. This second one is for the spouse's school run and grocery dash — tight budget, tiny footprint, and you'll happily take a manual petrol with basic safety to keep the spend low.
highlights: Sub ₹6L sticker keeps the household spend in check
Sub-4m hatchback for the tightest parking
Light controls for the second driver in the family
Cheap insurance and minimal running cost

title: Enthusiast driver
tagline: Driving feel comes first — boot can wait
description: You read every Autocar review and watch every PowerDrift video. You want a car with character — a punchy petrol, sharp steering, the option of a manual gearbox — even if it means a smaller boot and worse fuel economy than the spreadsheet says you should buy.
highlights: Manual or automatic — whichever drives better
4-star+ safety so the fun stays guilt-free

title: Retired comfort buyer
tagline: Easy in, easy out, soft over potholes
description: Retired and done with stick-shifts and bone-shaking suspension. You want a high-set seat for easy ingress, an automatic gearbox, a soft ride that takes the sting out of broken roads, and enough safety kit to give the family peace of mind.
highlights: High SUV seating for easy ingress / egress
Soft, absorbent ride over broken city roads
